channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No more cheap skirts: Trump ends tax exemption for low-value Chinese imports
item.description: A notice to customers dazzled by the low-priced products on Chinese shopping apps: the days of getting trendy clothing, tools and gag gifts that cost less than lunch delivered to your door in 10 days are probably numbered.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — At least six people have been killed in a wide swath of violent storms and tornadoes that hit the South and Midwest, and officials are bracing for more severe weather and flooding in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge says she will temporarily block billions in health funding cuts to states
item.description: A federal judge in Rhode Island says she'll temporarily block the Trump administration from cutting billions in federal public health dollars.
item.dc:creator: Devna Bose, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Remains of Illinois woman who disappeared in 1983 found after cold case investigators start podcast
item.description: Karen Schepers' remains were finally identified after being found in a car submerged in the Fox River, nearly 43 years after she went missing, thanks to the efforts of the Elgin Police Department and the non-profit organization Chaos Divers.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, KRON4

item.title: What to know about the Trump tariffs upending global trade and markets
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The trade war President Donald Trump promised has begun, threatening the world economy and straining the United States’ longstanding alliances in Europe and Asia.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge says US government may have 'acted in bad faith' as he weighs contempt over deportation order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that the Trump administration may have "acted in bad faith” by trying to rush Venezuelan migrants out of the country before a court could block their deportations to El Salvador. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Snatched pets and livestock deaths blamed on wolves prompt emergency in rural New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Commissioners in a rural New Mexico county say pets are being snatched from front yards and livestock are being maimed and killed by endangered Mexican gray wolves that seem to have no fear of humans, prompting them to declare a state of emergency.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers propose controversial medically-assisted suicide bill
item.description: Minnesota state lawmakers are re-introducing a controversial bill legalizing medically-assisted death, which is currently legal in 10 states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge declines to send Young Thug to prison over a viral post that sparked backlash
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge in Atlanta has declined prosecutors' request to revoke Young Thug 's probation and send him to prison. But the judge cautioned him over his social media use after his viral post sparked an online backlash against the district attorney's office.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could significantly impact California’s exports, such as automobiles, computer equipment and semiconductors, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Maine man shoots, kills mother, passing driver, wounds two others before taking own life
item.description: A Maine man took his own life after killing his mother as they were traveling in a vehicle together and another woman in a different vehicle, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MTV Video Music Awards head to CBS for the first time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The MTV Video Music Awards have a new home: This year, the music award show will be broadcast by CBS for the first time, organizers announced Thursday. The three-hour show will broadcast live on CBS on Sunday, Sept. 7, from the UBS Arena just outside New York City.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: White House postpones Saturday garden tours due to anti-Trump demonstrations
item.description: The White House is rescheduling one of its annual spring garden tour dates due to a "massive" anti-Trump demonstration.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: White House postpones garden tours due to anti-Trump demonstrations
item.description: The spring garden tours that were scheduled for Saturday have been pushed a day later, to Sunday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Yale fires legal scholar amid review of possible ties to 'sham charity' for designated terror group
item.description: Yale Law School has fired a scholar it says refused to appear in person to answer questions about her possible ties to organizations deemed to support terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What you do before and during a tornado could mean the difference between life and death
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — With new innovations and technology, scientists have learned from major tornado outbreaks over the years how to improve safety guidance. During tornado season, forecasters try to get the word out about staying safe during a storm — but also planning for it ahead of time.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, ABC 4

item.title: Tufts University declares support for student detained by ICE, seeks immediate release
item.description: The President of Tufts University filed a declaration in support of Rümeysa Öztürk, a student at the institution who is being detained by ICE. She was accused of supporting Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pence will get the Profile in Courage Award from JFK Library Foundation for his actions on Jan. 6
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence will receive the John F. Kennedy Profile in Courage Award for his refusal to go along with President Donald Trump’s efforts to remain in office after losing the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ICE releases man seeking life-saving transplant for brother after a month in custody
item.description: Jose Gregorio Gonzalez had been detained by ICE after he accompanied his brother, Jose Alfredo Gonzalez, to a kidney dialysis appointment.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tony's Chocolonely issues recall after 'small stones' found in chocolate bars
item.description: Tony's Chocolonely Inc. issued a voluntary recall for its Tony's brand Dark Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar because the products may contain small stones. The two bars were sold nationwide via retail stores and www.tonyschocolonely.com. The product was distributed from Feb. 7 to March 24.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: When heavy rainfall threatens flooding, you can protect yourself. Here's how
item.description: Extreme weather is dangerous weather, and that's particularly true for the heavy rainfall events that experts say are becoming more frequent with climate change. The powerful storms can pose threats ranging from falling limbs to downed power lines to drowning.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois homeschool bill would create a 'pipeline to the criminal justice system for parents': Dem lawmaker
item.description: An Illinois bill that could change homeschooling rules would create "a pipeline to the criminal justice system for parents," a state Democrat is warning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are eliminating diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wild weather expected Wednesday gets a rare 'high-risk' designation
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — U.S. government forecasters are using a relatively rare “high-risk” designation — the highest category they use — to warn that a major tornado outbreak appears likely Wednesday in an area that's home to about 2.5 million people.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades in Connecticut
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his "physical condition resembles one who would be at a point at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KRON4

item.title: Nintendo announces Switch 2 release date, new features in latest Direct showcase
item.description: After years of waiting, Nintendo fans finally have a release date for the Japanese company's new gaming console.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Chocolate bars recalled over possible small stones
item.description: Dutch chocolate maker Tony's Chocoloney has issued a nationwide recall of two of its chocolate products, which may contain small stones.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 launches in June with new 'Mario Kart World' game
item.description: Nintendo has announced a June 5 launch date for its latest gaming console, the Switch 2, which will introduce interactive chat and screenshare functions to connect gamers.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Average mortgage payments hit new record high
item.description: A typical monthly housing payment hit a record high of $2,807 during the last four weeks ending March 23, according to a study by Redfin. The 5.3% increase is a result of the average cost of home prices going up roughly 3% annually and mortgage rates sitting at 6.6%.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, KTLA 5

item.title: Monthly mortgage payments soar to record high
item.description: A typical monthly housing payment hit a record high during the last four weeks ending March 23, according to a study by Redfin.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, KRON4

item.title: Kidney donor detained by ICE before life-saving transplant can take place
item.description: Immigration rights activists are calling on ICE to release a Venezuelan migrant who was preparing to donate a kidney to his brother.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, Fox 5

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's in 17 states recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4
